summary: Kenneth Suna discusses portfolio rebalancing strategies based on age, recommending gradual shifts from growth assets (tech, S&P 500) toward bonds and dividends as investors approach retirement. The video focuses on long-term asset allocation and tax-advantaged account placement rather than options trading or income generation strategies.
takeaways: Ages 40-50: 40% technology, 30% S&P 500, 20% dividends, consider adding bonds
Place high-growth assets (tech) in Roth IRA to avoid tax bills on multi-hundred-percent gains over 30 years
Place bonds and dividends in individual taxable accounts
Rebalance slowly and gradually, not abruptly
By age 70, consider 50-60% bonds but maintain growth exposure
title: How much should you invest by AGE? 🚨 REALLOCATION EXPLAINED 🚨
